product_name: PQC Continuity: Downgrade Protection for TLS Servers Migrating to PQC (draft-sheffer-tls-pqc-continuity)
product_brief: A proposed TLS extension defining a mechanism for peers to cache commitments to present PQC or composite certificates.
pqc_capability_description: This is an Internet-Draft proposing a TLS extension (`pq_cert_available`) to prevent downgrade attacks during PQC migration. It allows clients to cache server commitments to use PQC algorithms (specifically referencing ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and composites). It is not a software product but a protocol specification under development.
crypto_primitives: ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, Composite signatures, Traditional certificates (legacy)
pqc_roadmap_details: Defines a mechanism for caching PQC commitments; references ongoing IETF work on ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and composite signatures.
signature_schemes: ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, Composite signatures, Traditional signature schemes (legacy)
